title: Max Area of Island
problem_statement: You are given an m x n binary matrix grid. An island is a group of 1's (representing land) connected 4-directionally (horizontal or vertical.) You may assume all four edges of the grid are surrounded by water. The area of an island is the number of cells with a value 1 in the island. Return the maximum area of an island in grid. If there is no island, return 0.

examples.explanation: The answer is not 11, because the island must be connected 4-directionally.

intuition: Traverse the grid, for each land cell, explore the connected component using DFS or BFS, count the area, and track the max.

approaches.name: DFS Flood Fill
approaches.description: Iterate through grid, when find 1, DFS to mark all connected 1s as visited (set to 0), count area.
approaches.why_not: BFS uses queue, DFS uses stack; both fine, DFS is recursive.
interview_commentary: DFS explores 4 directions, marks visited by setting to 0.
Returns the area of the island.

approaches.name: BFS Flood Fill
approaches.description: Use queue for BFS, start from each 1, explore neighbors, count area.
approaches.why_not: DFS is recursive, BFS iterative; both equivalent.
interview_commentary: BFS uses queue, processes level by level.

interview_qa.a: To mark visited without extra space.
Recursion depth for DFS could be issue, use BFS.

interview_qa.q: How to count islands instead of max area?
interview_qa.a: Increment counter each time start DFS/BFS.
